_comment: S1-a shared cross-language fixture for the canonical Claude agent-session UUID parser. The SAME fixture drives the TS twin (canonicalizeSessionId in observability.ts), the Bash twin (canonicalize_agent_session_id in common.sh), and the Python twin (canonicalize_agent_session_id in intel app/observability/langfuse_session.py). It is duplicated byte-for-byte into intel/tests/fixtures/. If any twin drifts from this grammar, the same Claude session id canonicalizes to two strings and splits the Langfuse Session; this fixture catches that drift in all three languages. expected null means the parser drops the value (no agent session). The None input (raw is None / null / unset) is asserted separately per language since JSON cannot distinguish an absent key from a null string here. The SOH case carries a real U+0001 byte via the JSON \u0001 escape so every parser decodes it identically.

cases.name: canonical lowercase dashed uuid passes unchanged
surrounding spaces and tabs are trimmed then lowercased
trailing control char (SOH) is rejected (not whitespace, not trimmed)
